caption: A presentation slide for a course on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" is shown, featuring a "Summary" section. The top of the slide remains constant, stating, "A heat exchanger is a device in which the cold is transferred from cold fluid to hot fluid." The NPTEL logo appears in the bottom left corner, and a footer identifies the instructor as "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay." As the sequence progresses, more text is added to the slide. A second bullet point appears, defining "Effectiveness $\epsilon$" as "a ratio of actual heat transfer that is occurring to the maximum possible heat transfer that can occur theoretically." Subsequently, a third line is added, stating, "It is a dimensionless number between 0 and 1." Finally, a fourth point is added regarding the Linde-Hampson cycle, including the formulas "$\epsilon = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_9}$ or $\epsilon = \frac{h_3 - h_2}{h_3 - h_2}$".
